displayTitle: Three Levers Between You and the Source

beats.narration: Radiography's core skill is exposure control: unshielded, a typical field source spends your whole annual dose limit in about six minutes at one meter.

payload.title: EXPOSURE CONTROL
payload.sub: The skill that makes radiography a career.

beats.narration: Dose equals dose rate times time. You attack it with three levers: time near the source, distance from it, shielding between.
Distance is the most powerful lever: radiation spreads out, so doubling distance drops the rate to one quarter. The meter confirms the boundary.
Shielding cuts the rate in half with each half value layer — for iridium one ninety-two, about five millimeters of lead. Stack three: one eighth.

payload.heading: Shielding: half-value layers
payload.note: EACH HVL HALVES THE RATE · 3 HVL = 1/8

beats.narration: Time, distance, shielding — then ALARA: as low as reasonably achievable. Limits are ceilings, not targets. Boring dose records, on purpose.
